title: Count of divisors
hints: Use the Smallest Prime Factor (SPF) array technique for efficient factorization.
Build SPF array using Sieve of Eratosthenes up to max(A).
For each number, use SPF to factorize it and count the exponents of each prime factor.
The number of divisors = (e1+1) * (e2+1) * ... * (ek+1) where e1, e2, ..., ek are exponents of prime factors.
For example, 12 = 2^2 * 3^1, so number of divisors = (2+1) * (1+1) = 6.
Use SPF to quickly find the smallest prime factor of any number during factorization.
